spec_match.rationale: Touches DBSC, device-bound-session-credentials from the chromestatus summary but skips web-platform — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "1. Login + register"; "2. Make API call"; "3. Force cookie expiry" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5140168270413824.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on cookie-lifecycle
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Device Bound Session Credentials introduces web-platform but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://w3c.github.io/webappsec-dbsc

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on cookie-lifecycle
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: cookie-lifecycle covers the happy path of Device Bound Session Credentials and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
